phase.name: Add loom research (verb + whiteboard-composed skill + recovery)

unit: /loom-research skill body — grill-me + shift detection + whiteboard + fact-check + recovery

contract.goal: Author the `/loom-research` skill body at `skills/loom-research/SKILL.md`. The skill is the research half of the RPI loop: grill-me interview with concrete domain-shift detection, whiteboard composition per shift (via /guild-whiteboard), evidence-anchored fact-check pass before commit (via /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit), human/auto duality, and recovery flow. Composes the two guild primitives and dispatches deterministic file IO through `bin/loom research` (D1's CLI verb). Documents the 6 skill-side event emissions and the griot [portable]-marker integration. Cites established conventions (RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape, two-budget defaults) rather than restating them.
acceptance_criteria: Frontmatter follows the marketplace skill convention (name, description, argument-hint, user-invocable, disable-model-invocation, allowed-tools).

Documents the domain-shift detection rule with both signals (≥40% content-word vocab delta + LLM-detected explicit focus-shift cue) ANDed, plus 3+ shift examples and 3+ non-shift counter-examples — PLAN.md 3.2 explicit requirement.

Documents the whiteboard composition per shift: full registered roster via /guild-whiteboard, engineers self-recuse off-topic, artifact path `projects/<slug>/whiteboards/research-shift-NN-<topic>.md`, raw contributions inline in RESEARCH-NOTES.md, synthesized signal in RESEARCH.md.
Documents the evidence-anchored fact-check pass via /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit against the candidate RESEARCH.md before commit.
Documents human/auto duality with the substrate two-budget defaults (3 rounds × 5 shifts, sourced from docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md).
Documents the recovery flow by citing the canonical RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape in docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md § Recovery from sub-agent failures — does NOT inline or restate the schema.

Documents the 6 skill-side event emissions (research-shift, research-panel-spawned, research-panel-verdict, research-fact-check-spawned, research-fact-check-verdict, research-budget-exhausted) at the corresponding decision points; the CLI-owned events (research-started, research-completed) are explicitly noted as not the skill's responsibility.

Documents griot integration explicitly via § Capture finding for both the per-shift [portable]-marker scan AND the hardcoded research-budget-exhausted write. The verb-shape gap (the current classification surface of `bin/griot capture` doesn't have a clean form for these captures) is named as a Phase 7 follow-up rather than papered over.
Cites `bin/loom research` for the deterministic file IO; explicitly forbids writing into `projects/<slug>/` directly.
rules_applied: CLAUDE.md: prefer citation over restatement — the RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape and the two-budget defaults live in docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md; the skill cites both, drift-proofing the skill against future schema updates.
CLAUDE.md: don't add features beyond the task — the skill DOES NOT add a `--mode=auto` arg to `bin/loom research`; auto-mode is a skill-runtime concern, the CLI stays auto-agnostic.
PLAN.md Phase 3 sub-section 3.2 dictates the skill's behavior verbatim; sub-section 3.4 dictates the griot integration.
Substrate composition contract: invoke `/guild-whiteboard` for the per-shift panel and `/guild-validate` for the fact-check gate. Never call the Agent tool directly from skill body.
disqualifiers: Re-implementing the RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape (it's documented in docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md; the skill cites it).
Adding `--mode=auto` to the CLI verb — auto-mode is skill-side only.
inputs: docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md:130-296 (Recovery + Auto-mode + two-budget conventions; canonical RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape lives here)
docs/SUBSTRATE-COMPOSITIONS.md:273-314 (§ Capture finding recipe — the griot integration pathway)
skills/draft-plan/SKILL.md (structural model for marketplace skill bodies)
skills/guild-whiteboard/SKILL.md (the composition primitive the skill calls per shift)
cli/verbs/research.ts (D1 of this phase — the CLI the skill delegates to)

actions: Authored skills/loom-research/SKILL.md (397 lines, then revised to ~405 after the round-2 fix). Structure: frontmatter → intro → Inputs → 7-step Process (Pre-flight + recovery; Frame; Grill-me + shift detection; Whiteboard composition per shift; Fact-check pass; Write temp files + commit via CLI; Report) → Human / auto duality → Recovery flow → Rules → Failure modes.

Documented the domain-shift rule with both signals (≥40% content-word vocab delta + LLM-detected focus-shift cue) ANDed, plus three shift examples (React useEffect → CSS tokens; CI cost → on-call rotation; Postgres index → sharding) and three non-shift counter-examples (useEffect → useLayoutEffect refinement; token pipeline → Figma export follow-up; CI conversation → pasted stack trace).

Documented the per-shift whiteboard composition: resolve full registered roster via Glob, invoke /guild-whiteboard, write attributed contributions to RESEARCH-NOTES.md, synthesize the signal into RESEARCH.md under `## Whiteboard contributions`.

Documented the fact-check pass: invoke /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit against the candidate RESEARCH.md before commit; flagged claims iterate (grill the user; auto-mode iterates panel-against-claim).
Cited docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md for the two-budget defaults (3 × 5 for /loom-research) and for the RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape; did NOT re-document either.
Listed the 6 skill-side event emission points; called out the CLI-owned (research-started, research-completed) emissions as out-of-skill by design.

Documented the griot integration: per-shift [portable]-marker scan + hardcoded budget-exhausted write, both via § Capture finding. Acknowledged the verb-shape gap (no clean classification for whiteboard-portable / budget-exhausted captures today) as a Phase 7 follow-up.
Authored the CLI delegation rule: all file IO goes through `bin/loom research`; never write into `projects/<slug>/` directly.
Ran `npm test`: 485/485 passed (skill body is markdown — no test surface change).

Derived evaluator panel via `bin/guild derive-panel --files=skills/loom-research/SKILL.md` → evaluator-contract-fit (substrate-only file, single evaluator per the loop's auto-derivation rules + the fallback panel-spec).

Invoked /guild-validate. Round 1 returned `flagged` with two findings: (1) the § Recovery flow section inlined the full RECOVERY-STATUS.json JSON shape after citing AGENT-CONVENTIONS § File shape, duplicating the canonical source; (2) the hardcoded research-budget-exhausted griot write said only 'records the failure pattern for griot ingestion' without explicitly naming § Capture finding the way the step-4 [portable] scan does.

Round-2 fix applied to § Recovery flow: removed the inline JSON block entirely; rewrote the section as 'cite + skill-specific field usage list + resume/removal pointer'. The skill names which values it puts in parent_skill, failed_step, resume_from, and context — a description of usage, not a re-declaration of the schema.

Round-2 fix applied to the hardcoded budget-exhausted paragraph: now explicitly invokes § Capture finding via the `bin/griot capture --evaluator-finding=<classification> ...` pathway. Documents the classification gap honestly (no precise match for 'budget-exhausted research session' today; `catalog-gap` once supported is the closest fallback) and names the gap as a Phase 7 follow-up.
Re-invoked /guild-validate (round 2). Verdict: approved. Both round-1 findings resolved; no new findings; no other sections touched so round-1 approvals on AC1-7 and AC9 still hold.
corrections: Round 1 evaluator finding: § Recovery flow inlined the RECOVERY-STATUS.json JSON shape despite the disqualifier saying 'cite, do not re-document'. The duplication would drift from the canonical source over time. Round-2 rewrite: cite-only, with a skill-specific field-usage list (what the skill puts in each field, not what shape the file has). Drift-proofed.

Round 1 evaluator finding: the hardcoded research-budget-exhausted griot write paragraph said 'records the failure pattern for griot ingestion' without explicitly naming § Capture finding, while the step-4 [portable] scan did name it. AC8 required both pathways to invoke § Capture finding. Round-2 fix: budget-exhausted paragraph now explicitly invokes the recipe via the `bin/griot capture --evaluator-finding=` pathway and documents the classification-surface gap as a Phase 7 follow-up. Intent recorded; verb extension is downstream.

changes_since_previous: Second unit on this branch. D1 (checkin 01) shipped the `loom research` CLI verb + 8 new event types in `cli/lib/types.ts`. D2 adds the skill body that orchestrates the grill-me interview, the per-shift whiteboard composition, the fact-check pass, and the human/auto duality on top of D1's CLI surface. The skill emits the 6 events D1 declared (research-shift, research-panel-spawned, research-panel-verdict, research-fact-check-spawned, research-fact-check-verdict, research-budget-exhausted); the CLI emits the other two (research-started, research-completed) from D1.
notes_for_pr: Round-1 evaluator finding led to a substantive rewrite of § Recovery flow: removed the inline JSON shape duplication and replaced with a cite-only paragraph + skill-specific field-usage description. The canonical RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape stays in docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md alone; the skill describes what it puts in each field, not what the schema is. Drift-proofs the skill against future schema updates.

Round-1 finding also tightened the hardcoded research-budget-exhausted griot write to explicitly invoke § Capture finding via the `bin/griot capture --evaluator-finding=<classification>` pathway. The verb's current classification surface (`recurring` + reserved-but-not-yet-implemented `catalog-gap` / `generator-antipattern` / `evaluator-conflict` / `sanctioned-exception`) doesn't have a precise match for 'budget-exhausted research session' or 'whiteboard-portable marker'. The skill documents the gap with a Phase 7 follow-up note — the integration intent is recorded; the verb extension is downstream work.

The skill is citation-heavy by design. Two-budget defaults (3 × 5), RECOVERY-STATUS.json shape, § Capture finding recipe all live elsewhere; the skill cites and uses them rather than restating. This matches Phase 1's substrate-doc design ethos.

Domain-shift detection rule is concrete: vocab delta ≥ 40% (set intersection / set union of content-word sets, last message vs prior 6) AND LLM-detected explicit reorientation cue. Both must fire for a shift. PLAN.md 3.2 dictated the 3+ shift / 3+ counter-example requirement; the skill body satisfies it with examples grounded in actual engineering domains (React lifecycle, CI cost, Postgres scaling).

Whiteboard composition is full-roster + self-recuse. The skill does NOT pre-filter engineers by domain at the spawn layer — it trusts each engineer to declare inapplicability when it doesn't apply, matching the evaluator non-applicability pattern documented in agents/evaluator-base.md and seen in this phase's evaluator-react-api responses for the CLI work.

The skill body is 405 lines after the round-2 fix. Slightly above the 200-line per-unit guidance (CLAUDE.md), but skill bodies are cohesive single-file artifacts where splitting would fracture the process narrative. draft-plan (234 lines), guild-whiteboard (299 lines) set the rough upper bound; loom-research's added behaviors (shift detection, fact-check pass, human/auto duality, recovery flow, griot integration) justify the larger surface.

Manual override applied per the loop's L-004 session-boundary constraint? No — no new evaluator agents were authored in this session, so the panel composition is unaffected. The panel ran with the existing evaluator-contract-fit agent registered well before session start.
